title: sambucus mexicana var. cerulea
intro: Sambucus mexicana var. cerulea, also known as the blue elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Mexico and parts of Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help combat infections and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of blue elderberry include reducing the severity and duration of respiratory infections, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Blue elderberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal remedies to boost the immune system and provide relief from seasonal allergies.
